name: BRELSFORD RES (DIANA)
address: 1312 NW DISCOVERY PARK DRIVE, BEND, OR 97703

fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the accessibility and movement for emergency responders could be achieved by ensuring that the property's single access point is clearly marked and unobstructed, facilitating swift entry. Adjustments to the fencing can create strategically placed openings or gates to support alternative entry and exit routes, promoting smoother responder flow. Maintaining clear pathways from the access point to the main building and across landscaped areas may assist in efficient navigation and staging. Streamlining pedestrian walkways with visible markings can serve as guides for responders, enhancing movement and visibility. By managing these elements, the property layout could better accommodate emergency vehicles and personnel during operations.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Defensible Space; Open Terrain; Scattered Shrubs; Limited Rear Access; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter
wildfire.recommendations: Improving the separation of vegetation adjacent to the structure and managing scattered shrubs and trees within the property's immediate perimeter could enhance defensible space and reduce fuel continuity. Emphasizing vegetation management in these areas, particularly around the structure, may reduce structure exposure and support long-term wildfire resilience. The limited rear access should be addressed by ensuring any vegetation does not impede potential firefighter ingress or egress, which could be further supported by maintaining clear access routes and open terrain. Preserving and maintaining the vegetated perimeter with an emphasis on reducing dense concentrations of combustible materials will help in creating and sustaining effective fuel breaks. This approach can collectively contribute to more robust property preparedness and support suppression efforts, potentially limiting fire spread through improved management of the existing vegetation layout.
